name.es: Pan de pepitas de calabaza de Estiria
name.fr: Pain aux graines de courge de Styrie

description.en: A Styrian farmhouse loaf built on a scalded pumpkin-seed soaker, a short rye sourdough and a dash of the region's dark green pumpkin seed oil. Rolled in seeds before baking, it slices moist and nutty, equally good under ham or soft cheese.
description.de: Ein steirisches Bauernbrot aus einem gebrühten Kürbiskernstück, kurzem Roggensauerteig und einem Schuss dunkelgrünem Kürbiskernöl. Vor dem Backen in Kernen gewälzt, schmeckt es saftig-nussig – ideal zu Speck oder würzigem Käse.
description.es: Un pan de campo de Estiria sobre un escaldado de pepitas de calabaza, una breve masa madre de centeno y un toque del aceite de semillas de calabaza verde oscuro típico de la región. Rebozado en pepitas, resulta jugoso y de sabor a nuez.
description.fr: Un pain de ferme styrien bâti sur une trempe de graines de courge ébouillantées, un bref levain de seigle et une touche d'huile de pépins de courge vert foncé, typique de la région. Roulé dans les graines, il est moelleux et parfumé de noisette.

origin.note: Styrian pumpkin seed oil (Steirisches Kürbiskernöl) holds EU protected-origin status; this bread folds the region's signature oil and seeds into an everyday sourdough loaf.

ingredients.note: dark rye, Type 1150, for the sourdough
550g boiling, scalded over the soaker; 120g in the sourdough build
active culture to build the rye sourdough
roasted, for the soaker and rolling

yield.shape: small tins, 22x9x6cm, rolled in pumpkin seeds

keeping.note: Keeps 3-4 days in a cloth bag or breadbox; freezes well for up to two months.

method.title: Scald the soaker
method.summary: Pour boiling water over spelt wholemeal flour and roasted pumpkin seeds, stir well, cover and rest overnight.

method.title: Build the rye sourdough
method.summary: Combine dark rye flour, water and a little active culture; ferment covered at room temperature 8-12 hours.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine the soaker, the sourdough, the white flour, pumpkin seed oil, fresh yeast, salt and curd cheese; mix briefly then knead until smooth, about 8 minutes.

method.title: Bulk ferment
method.summary: Let the dough rise about 90 minutes, folding once partway through with wet hands.

method.title: Divide and shape
method.summary: Divide into four pieces, roll each in pumpkin seeds, shape into short loaves and settle into greased tins.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Cover and let rise 45-60 minutes until domed.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake with steam at high heat, then vent and finish at a lower temperature until deeply colored, about 30 minutes total.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Unmold immediately and cool on a rack before slicing.

source.adaptation: Method paraphrased; ingredient grams kept at the source's stated 4-loaf batch size rather than scaled to a single loaf.
